item.title: 🤝 Merger · EBAY
item.ns0:headline: Proposes $125/share cash+stock acquisition of eBay
item.description:
Proposes $125/share cash+stock acquisition of eBay

GameStop Corp., led by Ryan Cohen, proposes to acquire eBay Inc. for $125 per share in a combination of cash and GameStop common stock. GameStop owns 25,000 shares and has put/call options for 23,176,000 additional shares. The non-binding proposal is subject to HSR clearance and eBay board engagement.

About: eBay Inc. operates marketplace platforms that connect buyers and sellers in the United States and internationally. The company's Marketplace platform includes its online marketplace at ebay.com and the eBay suite of mobile apps. Its platforms enable users to list, buy, sell, and pay for items through various online, mobile, and offline channels that include retailers, distributors, liquidators, import and export companies, auctioneers, catalog and mail-order companies, directories, search engine…

  • Counterparty: GameStop Corp.
  • Offer price: $125
  • Consideration: cash+stock
  • Announced: 2026-05-14

item.title: 🤝 Merger · GORO
item.ns0:headline: Stock merger with Goldgroup Mining; 1.4476x exchange ratio; special meeting vote pending
item.description:
Stock merger with Goldgroup Mining; 1.4476x exchange ratio; special meeting vote pending

Shareholders will receive 1.4476 Goldgroup Mining shares per GORO share in a reverse triangular merger, with GORO surviving as a wholly owned subsidiary. GORO shareholders are expected to own approximately 40% of the combined company. The merger reflects a 39% premium to GORO's closing price on January 23, 2026, and a special meeting vote is pending.

About: Gold Resource Corporation engages in the exploration, development, and production of gold and silver projects in Mexico and the United States. The company also explores for copper, lead, and zinc deposits. Its principal asset is the 100% owned Back Forty project covering approximately 1,304 hectares located in Menominee county, Michigan. The company was founded in 1998 and is headquartered in Denver, Colorado.

  • Counterparty: Goldgroup Mining Inc.
  • Premium: 39%
  • Consideration: stock
  • Announced: 2026-01-25

item.ns0:headline: Sells commercial product portfolio to Apotex for $100M cash; vote pending
item.description:
Sells commercial product portfolio to Apotex for $100M cash; vote pending

Agreed to sell its FDA-approved commercial products (Acetadote, Caldolor, Kristalose, Sancuso, Vaprisol, Vibativ, Talicia) and related assets to an affiliate of Apotex Inc. for $100 million in cash. The company will retain its ifetroban product candidates and CET subsidiary. The transaction requires shareholder approval at a special meeting and is expected to close in Q2/Q3 2026.

item.title: 🤝 Merger · MPX
item.ns0:headline: Acquired by MasterCraft for 0.232 shares + $2.43 cash; closed May 15
item.description:
Acquired by MasterCraft for 0.232 shares + $2.43 cash; closed May 15

Corporation was acquired by MasterCraft Boat Holdings in a two-step merger completed May 15, 2026. Each Marine Products share converted into 0.232 MasterCraft common shares plus $2.43 cash, with fractional shares paid in cash based on a $25.27 VWAP. Marine Products common stock was delisted from NYSE and deregistered.

About: Marine Products Corporation designs, manufactures, and sells recreational fiberglass powerboats for the sportboat, sport fishing, and jet boat markets worldwide. The company offers Chaparral sterndrive pleasure boats, including SSi Sport Boats, SSX Sport Boats, and the Surf Series; Chaparral outboard pleasure boats, which include OSX Luxury Sportboats, and SSi and SSX outboard models; and Robalo outboard sport fishing boats.

  • Offer price: 0.232 shares + $2.43 cash
  • Consideration: cash+stock
  • Announced: 2026-02-05
  • Expected Close: 2026-05-15

item.title: 📉 Delisting · Forced · RANI
item.ns0:headline: Forced Nasdaq delisting notice; 180-day cure period ends Nov 9
item.description:
Forced Nasdaq delisting notice; 180-day cure period ends Nov 9

Received a Nasdaq notice on May 11, 2026, for failing to maintain the minimum $1.00 bid price for 30 consecutive business days. The company has 180 days, until November 9, 2026, to regain compliance by closing at $1.00 or more for 10 consecutive days. If not cured, it may transfer to the Nasdaq Capital Market for an additional 180-day period.

About: Rani Therapeutics Holdings, Inc. operates as a clinical stage biotherapeutics company that develops orally administered biologics. The company develops the RaniPill capsule, a platform that is intended to replace subcutaneous or IV injection of biologics with oral dosing. Its product pipeline includes RT-101, an octreotide, which has completed Phase I clinical trial for the treatment of neuroendocrine tumors and acromegaly; RT-105, an anti-TNF-alpha antibody to treat psoriatic arthritis; RT-102,…

item.title: ⚖️ Litigation · Fda Approval · ONC
item.ns0:headline: FDA grants accelerated approval for BEQALZI in R/R MCL
item.description:
FDA grants accelerated approval for BEQALZI in R/R MCL

Received FDA accelerated approval for BEQALZI (sonrotoclax) for relapsed/refractory mantle cell lymphoma after ≥2 prior therapies including a BTK inhibitor. Approval based on Phase 1/2 study showing 52% ORR, 16% CR, median DOR 15.8 months. Continued approval contingent on confirmatory CELESTIAL-RRMCL trial.

About: BeOne Medicines, formerly known as BeiGene, is a global oncology company focused on discovering, developing, and commercializing innovative cancer therapies. Founded in 2010 and headquartered in Cambridge, Massachusetts—with operations spanning over 45 countries across six continents—the company rebranded as BeOne in late 2024 and redomiciled to Basel, Switzerland in 2025.

  • Announced: 2026-05-13

item.title: 🤝 Merger · TBH
item.ns0:headline: Extends merger deadline with House of Doge to June 30, 2026
item.description:
Extends merger deadline with House of Doge to June 30, 2026

Entered into Amendment No. 4 to its Merger Agreement with House of Doge Inc., extending the outside termination date from a prior date to June 30, 2026. The merger, originally announced October 12, 2025, will see Merger Sub merge into House of Doge, with House of Doge surviving as a wholly owned subsidiary. No other terms were modified.

About: Brag House Holdings, Inc. operates an integrated electronic video game sports or esports platform for casual gamers. It hosts esports tournaments, including the streaming and live broadcast of tournaments. The company also offers in-game stat-based predictions, including lifestyle and behavior insights, and predictive analytics to brands; and advertising and marketing services.

  • Counterparty: House of Doge Inc.
  • Announced: 2025-10-12

item.description:
Amends merger agreement; GRC holders to own 40% of combined company

Goldgroup Mining Inc. amends its arrangement agreement with Gold Resource Corp (GORO) to replace the fixed 4:1 share consolidation ratio with a ratio to be determined jointly and approved by TSXV. Upon closing, GRC stockholders will own approximately 40% of the combined company on a fully-diluted basis, with Goldgroup shareholders holding 60%. The parties also announced anticipated board nominees for the combined entity, including Ron Little, Lila Manassa Murphy, Nicole Adshead-Bell, Luis Felipe Medina Aguirre, and Francisco Javier Reyes de la Campa.

About: Gold Resource Corporation engages in the exploration, development, and production of gold and silver projects in Mexico and the United States. The company also explores for copper, lead, and zinc deposits. Its principal asset is the 100% owned Back Forty project covering approximately 1,304 hectares located in Menominee county, Michigan. The company was founded in 1998 and is headquartered in Denver, Colorado.

item.ns0:headline: Acquired by GIP/EQT for $15.00 cash per share; special meeting June 26
item.description:
Acquired by GIP/EQT for $15.00 cash per share; special meeting June 26

Stockholders will receive $15.00 per share in cash in a take-private merger with a consortium led by Global Infrastructure Partners and EQT Infrastructure VI. The special meeting to vote on the merger is scheduled for June 26, 2026. The offer represents a 35.5% premium over the unaffected closing price.

About: The AES Corporation operates as a diversified power generation and utility company. It owns and/or operates power plants to generate and sell power to customers, such as utilities, industrial users, and other intermediaries. The company also owns and/or operates utilities to generate or purchase, distribute, transmit, and sell electricity to end-user customers in the residential, commercial, industrial, and governmental sectors; and generates and sells electricity on the wholesale market.

item.title: 📤 Divestiture · SSP
item.ns0:headline: Swaps stations with Gray Media; no cash consideration
item.description:
Swaps stations with Gray Media; no cash consideration

Scripps completed a station swap with Gray Media on May 15, 2026. Scripps acquired KKTV (CBS) in Colorado Springs, KKCO (NBC) and KJCT-LP (ABC) in Grand Junction, and KMVT (CBS) and KSVT-LD (Fox) in Twin Falls. Gray acquired WSYM (Fox) in Lansing and KATC (ABC) in Lafayette. The exchange was even, with no cash paid by either party.

About: The E.W. Scripps Company, together with its subsidiaries, operates as a media enterprise through a portfolio of local and national media brands. The company operates through Local Media, Scripps Network, and Other segments. The Local Media segment operates broadcast television stations, which produce news, information, and entertainment content, as well as its related digital operations. item.description:

Entered into a merger agreement on March 2, 2026, under which Merger Sub will merge with and into the company. Each outstanding share (excluding rollover shares and appraisal shares) will be converted into the right to receive $16.50 in cash. The transaction is financed by an $880M equity commitment from WCAS XIV, L.P. and up to $1B in debt financing. The special committee of independent directors unanimously recommended the deal, and the board approved it. The merger requires approval by a majority of outstanding shares, excluding shares held by affiliates and rollover holders. A special meeting of stockholders will be held to vote on the adoption of the merger agreement.

About: Select Medical Holdings Corporation, through its subsidiaries, operates critical illness recovery hospitals, rehabilitation hospitals, outpatient rehabilitation clinics, and occupational health centers in the United States. The acquisition adds the ALICE tethered drone and METRON photogrammetry system, completing Nanoveu's vertical integration from silicon to airframe. No financial terms were disclosed; the deal is subject to customary closing conditions.

About: Nanoveu Limited, a technology company, develops and sells nanotechnology applications for consumer devices in the Americas and internationally. The company provides Nanoshield, an antiviral protector, which is available in various mobile phone screen covers and cases, as well as a commercial film for various surface applications. It also offers EyeFly3D that converts 2D digital displays into 3D without the need for 3D glasses.

item.description:

GameStop Corp., led by Ryan Cohen, made an unsolicited $55 billion bid for eBay, offering half cash and half stock, representing a 46% premium to eBay's price when GameStop began accumulating shares. eBay's board rejected the proposal as not credible, citing financing uncertainty and operational risks. Cohen defends the bid, citing $9.4B cash on hand, up to $20B in debt financing from TD Securities, and $2B in planned cost cuts; the bid remains outstanding.

About: eBay Inc. operates marketplace platforms that connect buyers and sellers in the United States and internationally. item.description:

item.title: 🔐 Going Private · KW
item.ns0:headline: $1.8B notes priced for McMorrow-led take-private; Fairfax backstops redemption
item.description:
$1.8B notes priced for McMorrow-led take-private; Fairfax backstops redemption

Kennedy-Wilson subsidiary priced $1.8B senior notes (7.000% due 2031 and 7.250% due 2033) to refinance existing notes and fund the pending take-private merger led by CEO William McMorrow and Fairfax Financial. If the merger closes, proceeds will redeem 4.750% notes due 2029/2030 and purchase 5.000% notes due 2031; if the merger fails by November 16, 2026, notes are subject to special mandatory redemption at par plus accrued interest, with Fairfax funding any shortfall. The merger remains subject to stockholder approval and other customary conditions.

About: Kennedy-Wilson Holdings, Inc., together with its subsidiaries, operates as a real estate investment company. The company owns, operates, and invests in real estate both on its own and through its investment management platform. It focuses on multifamily and office properties located in the Western United States, the United Kingdom, Ireland, Spain, Italy, and Japan.

  • Counterparty: McMorrow/Fairfax consortium
  • Announced: 2026-02-16
  • Expected Close: 2026-11-16

item.ns0:headline: C$44/share cash bid from Zijin Gold; regulatory approvals pending, outside date May 29
item.description:
C$44/share cash bid from Zijin Gold; regulatory approvals pending, outside date May 29

Zijin Gold International Company Limited agreed to acquire all shares of Allied Gold for C$44 per share in cash, valuing the company at approximately C$5.5 billion. All shareholder and court approvals have been obtained; regulatory approvals are pending, with an outside closing date of May 29, 2026, subject to extension. The transaction is structured as a court-approved plan of arrangement under Ontario law.

About: Allied Gold Corporation, together with its subsidiaries, explores and produces mineral deposits in Africa. The company primarily explores for gold and silver ores. Its flagship project is the Sadiola gold project, an open pit mine located in the Republic of Mali. The company holds interest in the Bonikro and Hiré mines located in Côte d'Ivoire; the Agbaou mine located in Côte d'Ivoire; and the Kurmuk gold project located in Ethiopia.

  • Counterparty: Zijin Gold International Company Limited

  • Consideration: cash

item.description:
BBRC seeks vote AGAINST Chair Donna James at VSCO annual meeting

BBRC International, a 13% shareholder of Victoria's Secret & Co., is soliciting proxies to vote AGAINST the reelection of Chair Donna James at the 2026 annual meeting. BBRC criticizes her 25-year tenure, citing poor capital allocation, failed acquisitions, and oversight failures related to the Les Wexner-Jeffrey Epstein relationship. The activist has filed a preliminary proxy statement and urges governance reform.

About: Victoria's Secret & Co. operates as a specialty retailer of women's intimate, personal care, and beauty products worldwide. The company offers bras, panties, lingerie, sleepwear, loungewear, and athletic attire and swimwear, as well as fragrances and body care products, and accessories under the Victoria's Secret and PINK brands. As of March 2, 2022, it operated approximately 1,400 retail stores. The company was incorporated in 2021 and is headquartered in Reynoldsburg, Ohio.

  • Counterparty: BBRC International PTE Limited

item.ns0:headline: Phase 2B/3 MIRACLE trial unblinding expected before June 30; blinded CR rate ~30% vs 17-18% historical
item.description:
Phase 2B/3 MIRACLE trial unblinding expected before June 30; blinded CR rate ~30% vs 17-18% historical

Announced imminent unblinding of first 45 subjects from its pivotal Phase 2B/3 MIRACLE trial evaluating Annamycin + cytarabine in relapsed/refractory AML. Preliminary blinded data show a complete remission rate of approximately 30%, compared to historical 17-18% for cytarabine alone. Unblinding is expected before June 30, 2026, with Part A recruitment on track to reach 90 subjects in Q3 2026.

item.description:

Agnico Eagle will acquire all Rupert Resources shares not already owned via a plan of arrangement. Each share receives 0.0401 Agnico Eagle common shares plus a contingent value right (CVR) of up to $3.00 cash. The special meeting to approve the arrangement is scheduled for June 9, 2026; the board unanimously recommends voting for the deal, which represents a 67% premium to the pre-announcement closing price.

About: Rupert Resources Ltd. engages in the acquisition and exploration of mineral properties in Finland. It primarily focuses on 100% owned Rupert Lapland Project Area including Ikkari discovery and Pahtavaara mine and mill covering an area of 595km2 located in Northern Finland. Rupert Resources Ltd. is headquartered in Toronto, Canada.

About: Expensify, Inc. provides a cloud-based expense management software platform to individuals, small businesses, and corporations in the United States and internationally. The company was founded in 2008 and is based in Portland, Oregon.

item.ns0:headline: Nasdaq delisting for bid price, late 10-K; hearing by May 13
item.description:
Nasdaq delisting for bid price, late 10-K; hearing by May 13

Received a Nasdaq Staff Determination on May 6, 2026, that its common stock will be delisted from the Nasdaq Capital Market due to non-compliance with the minimum bid price rule (below $1.00 for 30 consecutive business days) and failure to file its Annual Report on Form 10-K for 2025. The company intends to request a hearing before an independent panel by May 13, 2026, and is considering a reverse stock split to regain compliance. Trading will continue under the ticker AGAE pending the hearing outcome.

It operates esports properties to connect players and fans through a network of connected arenas; a flagship gaming arena located at the Luxor Hotel in Las Vegas, Nevada; a mobile esports truck that serves as a battleground and content generation hub; and a studio for recording and streaming gaming events. The company was formerly known as Allied Esports Entertainment Inc.

item.title: ⚖️ Litigation · Fda Pre Bla Meeting Positive · MLTX
item.ns0:headline: FDA aligns on sonelokimab BLA for HS; submission Sept 2026, launch H2 2027
item.description:
FDA aligns on sonelokimab BLA for HS; submission Sept 2026, launch H2 2027

Announced positive outcome from final pre-BLA meeting with FDA for sonelokimab (SLK) in hidradenitis suppurativa (HS). FDA aligned on submission plans and label strategy, including acceptability of MIRA trial data for effectiveness and inclusion of adolescent data from VELA-TEEN. BLA submission planned for end of September 2026; acceptance and Priority Review decision expected by end of November 2026; first commercial launch targeted for H2 2027 if approved. Company also reported Q1 2026 cash of $357.9M.

About: MoonLake Immunotherapeutics, a clinical-stage biopharmaceutical company, engages in developing therapies. It is developing Sonelokimab, a novel investigational Nanobody therapy for the treatment of inflammation. The company is involved in conducting Phase II trials for hidradenitis suppurativa, psoriatic arthritis, ankylosing spondylitis, or radiographic axial spondyloarthritis. MoonLake Immunotherapeutics was founded in 2021 and is headquartered in Zug, Switzerland.

  • Announced: 2026-05-10

item.description:
Forced Nasdaq delisting May 6; appeal hearing requested

Received a Staff Determination from Nasdaq on May 6, 2026, that its common stock will be delisted from The Nasdaq Capital Market due to the bid price closing below $1.00 per share for 30 consecutive business days (March 24, 2026 through May 5, 2026). The company is ineligible for a 180-day compliance period because it has effected reverse stock splits over the prior two years with a cumulative ratio of 250 shares or more to one. Aditxt intends to timely request a hearing before a Nasdaq Hearings Panel to appeal the determination, which will stay delisting pending the hearing outcome.

Telephone and Data Systems, Inc. (TDS), which already owns 81.9% of the outstanding stock and 95.9% of the voting interests, submitted a non-binding proposal on May 7, 2026 to acquire all remaining common shares of Array Digital Infrastructure. Array's board formed a special committee of independent directors, which retained PJT Partners as financial advisor and Cravath, Swaine & Moore as legal counsel, to evaluate the proposal. No price or definitive terms have been disclosed, and there is no assurance a transaction will occur.

Neugebauer 2020 Trust, is soliciting agent designations to call a special meeting of shareholders. Diana Shipping Inc., through its subsidiary 4 Dragon Merger Sub Inc., is making a cash tender offer to acquire all outstanding shares of Genco Shipping & Trading Ltd. at $23.50 per share, representing a 31% premium to the undisturbed price. Diana, which owns 14.7% of Genco, has also filed a definitive proxy statement to elect six independent director nominees at Genco's 2026 annual meeting on June 18, 2026. The tender offer is fully financed and remains subject to conditions.

It charters its vessels primarily to trading houses, including commodities traders; producers; and government-owned entities.

item.title: 🪙 Debt Restr. · Out-Of-Court Amendment · MERC
item.ns0:headline: Amends €370M RCF with UniCredit; waives leverage covenant, caps capex €60M
item.description:
Amends €370M RCF with UniCredit; waives leverage covenant, caps capex €60M

's German subsidiaries entered into a Waiver and Consent Request Letter with lenders to amend their €370.1 million revolving credit facility. The waiver suspends the leverage ratio covenant (max 3.50:1.00) for the first three quarters of fiscal 2026, restricts distributions to the parent (up to €15M aggregate with conditions), caps 2026 capex at €60M, and requires additional security over assets worth at least 110% of drawn amounts. The interest rate margin was modified to 2.50%-4.25% based on leverage levels.

About: Mercer International Inc., together with its subsidiaries, manufactures and sells northern bleached softwood kraft (NBSK) pulp in Europe, the United States, Asia, and internationally. The company operates through two segments, Pulp and Wood Products. It also generates and sells green energy produced from biomass cogeneration power plant to third party utilities. In addition, the company manufactures, distributes, and sells lumber and other wood residuals.

  • Counterparty: UniCredit Bank GmbH (as agent)

item.description:

Diana Shipping Inc., holder of 14.7% of Genco Shipping & Trading Ltd, is soliciting proxies to elect six director nominees at Genco's 2026 annual meeting on June 18, 2026. Diana, in partnership with Star Bulk, has made an increased offer of $23.50 per share in cash for Genco (31% premium to Nov 21, 2025 close), supported by $1.433 billion in committed financing, but the Genco board has refused to engage. The proxy contest aims to replace the board to facilitate a sale or other value-maximizing transaction.

About: Genco Shipping & Trading Limited, together with its subsidiaries, engages in the ocean transportation of dry bulk cargoes worldwide. The company owns and operates dry bulk carrier vessels to transports iron ore, coal, grains, steel products, and other dry-bulk cargoes. It charters its vessels primarily to trading houses, including commodities traders; producers; and government-owned entities.

item.title: 🤝 Merger · GLPG
item.description:
Galapagos entered into a binding Framework Agreement with Gilead in connection with Gilead's acquisition of Ouro Medicines; Galapagos will assume substantially all of Ouro's operating assets and personnel, and the parties will equally split the $1.675 billion upfront payment. The transaction is expected to close in Q2 2026, with Galapagos eligible for tiered royalties of 20%–23% on net sales of gamgertamig and up to $500 million in contingent milestones. Galapagos also has up to $500 million of cash available for strategic transactions, including up to $150 million for potential share repurchases.

About: Galapagos NV, an integrated biopharmaceutical company, engages in the discovery, development, and commercialization of various medicines for high unmet medical need. Its pipeline products include filgotinib, a JAK1 inhibitor that is in various phases of clinical trials for the treatment of rheumatoid arthritis, Crohn's disease, ulcerative colitis, small bowel CD, fistulizing CD, ankylosing spondylitis, psoriatic arthritis, and uveitis.

  • Counterparty: Gilead Sciences, Inc.
  • Deal value: $1.675 billion upfront plus up to $500 million in milestones
  • Consideration: cash
  • Announced: 2026-03-31
  • Expected Close: Q2 2026

item.title: 💧 Capital Return · Self Tender · SLM
item.description:
Commenced a cash tender offer to purchase any and all of its outstanding $500 million 3.125% senior notes due 2026. The purchase price will be determined by a fixed spread of +25 bps over the yield of the 4.125% U.S. Treasury due October 31, 2026, as quoted on May 12, 2026. The tender offer expires on May 12, 2026, with settlement expected on May 15, 2026, and is conditioned upon the completion of a concurrent new notes offering.

About: SLM Corporation, through its subsidiaries, originates and services private education loans to students and their families to finance the cost of their education in the United States. It also offers retail deposit accounts, including certificates of deposit, money market deposit accounts, and high-yield savings accounts; and omnibus accounts, as well as credit card loans. It serves students and families through financial aid, federal loans, and student and family resources.

  • Counterparty: SLM Corp (issuer)
